title: Corynostylis rufotomentosa
intro: Corynostylis rufotomentosa, also known as the 'Red-clothed Corynostylis', is a species of flowering plant in the family Pentaphyllaceae. Traditionally, Corynostylis rufotomentosa has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include phenolic compounds, such as gallic acid and ferulic acid, as well as flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Corynostylis rufotomentosa is typically used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to alleviate respiratory issues, while some studies suggest its potential use in the development of pharmaceutical products.
